fixture.description: toLowerCase() is called without a Locale on a value used as a map key; under a locale such as Turkish this maps ASCII letters differently and breaks the lookup.

grader.rubric: PASS if the report flags that countryCode.toLowerCase() is called WITHOUT an explicit Locale before being used as a map key, so under a locale such as Turkish (the dotless-i / dotted-I case mapping) the lookup can produce the wrong key and route incorrectly, and recommends toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT) (or Locale.ENGLISH). Severity DEBT, IMPORTANT, or SUGGESTED. FAIL if the locale-dependent case-conversion bug is not surfaced.

expected_behavior: A11y/i18n or Bugs agent flags locale-dependent toLowerCase used as a key
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT flag it as a hardcoded user-facing string (different issue)
